ops.title: Routing v4: implementation lane split to gpt-5.6-sol (research-backed, 2026-07-13)
ops.body:
**Decision** (user-approved after deep research): the implementation lane splits by task shape. Well-scoped, clearly-bounded implementation — terminal/shell-heavy execution included, net-new code when boundaries are crisp — routes to gpt-5.6-sol xhigh/fast via the codex skill; ambiguous or exploratory work, large multi-file refactors, and long agentic runs stay on opus-4.8 xhigh. **No fable lane moves.** Ultra execution mode is now CLI-invocable (codex 0.144.0 fixed the 400; local 0.144.1) but is NOT a retry rung — worst scope discipline of any config at 3–5x cost; a hard sol miss still escalates to fable.

**Evidence highlights**: sol leads AA Coding Agent Index (80 vs fable 77) and Terminal-Bench (88.8%), ~10% cheaper/task than opus and much faster; opus wins SWE-bench Pro real-repo (69.2 vs 64.6) and tau2 tool-use (94.4 vs 85.1); sol has the highest reward-hacking rate METR has recorded plus live codex-exec caveats (read-only sandbox default, Responses-Lite tool omission #31894, ~353K effective context cap #31860). Practitioner consensus: "Sol executes, Fable directs" — sol last-of-6 on standalone prose. codex `service_tier=fast` maps to the API priority tier (confirmed live via a bogus-model error event).

**Shipped**: cc-skills 69ec1d98 (claude-rules split + gitignore backstops) + 410f5d72 (codex 0.13.0 quiet exec — every exec pins `--json --color never`, redirects to a scratchpad log, surfaces only REPLY_FILE:/LOG_FILE: or a tail -20 failure excerpt; non-improvisable scratchpad fallback `S=$(mktemp -d)`, never repo-relative; repo-bootstrap 0.49.0 test pins) + 0722c9e1 (render); captain-hook general pack 0.20.0 (nudge messages + judge rubrics split-aware). Fleet CLAUDE.md rolls out via the daily guides cron. Scratchpad-leak root cause: an agent improvised `.claude-scratch/` instead of the documented mktemp fallback; skill prose was innocent.
